metadata.title: Texas A&M University System Operational Technology Control Standards

party.name: Texas A&M University System Office of Cybersecurity

part.name: ot-guidance
part.p: The policy specifically addresses the unique properties and requirements of OT and the relationship to non-OT systems. OT access by vendors and maintenance staff can occur over a large facility footprint or geographic area and into unobserved spaces, such as mechanical or electrical rooms, ceilings, floors, field substations, switch and valve vaults, and pump stations.

part.name: ot-guidance
part.p: In OT systems, physical security, personnel security, intrusion detection, or auditing measures may support this control objective.

part.name: ot-guidance
part.p: When the OT (e.g., field devices) cannot support temporary or emergency accounts, this enhancement does not apply. Example compensating controls include employing nonautomated mechanisms or procedures.

part.name: ot-guidance
part.p: This control enhancement defines situations or timeframes in which users log out of accounts in the policy. Automatic enforcement is not addressed by this control enhancement. Organizations determine whether this control enhancement is appropriate for the mission and/or functions of the OT system and define the timeframe or scenarios. If no timeframe or scenarios apply, the organization-defined parameter reflects as much.

part.name: ot-guidance
part.p: Close coordination occurs between OT, HR, IT, and physical security personnel to ensure the timely removal of high-risk individuals.

resource.title: Texas Security Control Standards Catalog Profile
